records.ety: [Gr. panopli`a; pa^s, pa^n, all + "o`plon tool, implement, in pl., armor, arms.]

defs: Defensive armor in general; a full suit of defensive armor.
quotes: We had need to take the Christian panoply, to put on the whole armor of God.

defs: Any full set of elaborate attire, complete with accessories or accompanying paraphernalia; any elaborate special or ceremonial attire and equipment.
Any impressive complete array; as, the full panoply of a presidential funeral.
Any complete array of devices used in an endeavor; as, to deploy the full panoply of writer's techniques.
